title: Capture of Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon is both a strategic asset and a historical burden for Israel
subhed: Twenty-six years after their withdrawal, Israeli soldiers entered the Crusader fortress overlooking the Litani River on Sunday. The move provided a new bargaining chip in negotiations with the Lebanese, which are set to resume in Washington on June 2 and 3.

title: Far-right leaders Bardella and Le Pen diverge on pension reform: 'It's the party's explosive issue'

Upcoming G7 in Evian puts serious strain on France-Switzerland relations
subhed: The summit, scheduled to take place on the French shore of Lake Geneva from June 15 to 17, has required the involvement of Swiss authorities due to delegations transiting through Geneva Airport and a planned demonstration in the city.

title: After cadmium, French doctors warn of drinking water pollution
subhed: In an open letter to the government, the National Conference of Regional Unions of Health Professionals highlights the threat posed by drinking water pollution from pesticides, PFAS and microplastics. The Medical Research Foundation also calls on public authorities to recognize the links between health and the environment.

title: 'It is urgent to heed the warning raised by Pope Leo XIV's encyclical on AI'
subhed: The fact that it took a spiritual authority to launch the debate on protecting humanity says a lot about the weakening of political power, which is struggling to rise to the civilizational challenges posed by artificial intelligence, writes Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

title: Mutual mistrust persists between football and the French far right
subhed: The Rassemblement National has made headlines more for its criticism and racist outbursts toward the national team than for its support during major tournaments. It has also failed to secure high-profile allies within the sport.

subhed: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.

title: Sheikha Al-Mayassa Al Thani, the princess spearheading Qatar's artistic ambitions
subhed: A central figure in the country's cultural policy, the emir's sister has skillfully used the acquisition of prestigious works of art, the hosting of fairs and the creation of museums to build valuable diplomatic ties.

title: The oil crisis suits TotalEnergies' CEO perfectly
subhed: Between price caps at the pump and controversies over windfall profits, Patrick Pouyané is once again at the center of public debate. Despite the tensions, he dreams of proving that the multinational company serves the French people's interest.
